pinpoint.campaign.AdditionalTreatments.Docs: An array of requests that defines additional treatments for the campaign, in addition to the default treatment for the campaign.

pinpoint.campaign.ApplicationId.Docs: The unique identifier for the Amazon Pinpoint application that the campaign is associated with.

pinpoint.campaign.CampaignHook.Docs: Specifies the Lambda function to use as a code hook for a campaign.

pinpoint.campaign.Description.Docs: A custom description of the campaign.

pinpoint.campaign.HoldoutPercent.Docs: The allocated percentage of users (segment members) who shouldn't receive messages from the campaign.

pinpoint.campaign.IsPaused.Docs: Specifies whether to pause the campaign. A paused campaign doesn't run unless you resume it by changing this value to `false`. If you restart a campaign, the campaign restarts from the beginning and not at the point you paused it.

pinpoint.campaign.Limits.Docs: The messaging limits for the campaign.

pinpoint.campaign.MessageConfiguration.Docs: The message configuration settings for the campaign.

pinpoint.campaign.Priority.Docs: An integer between 1 and 5, inclusive, that represents the priority of the in-app message campaign, where 1 is the highest priority and 5 is the lowest. If there are multiple messages scheduled to be displayed at the same time, the priority determines the order in which those messages are displayed.

pinpoint.campaign.Schedule.Docs: The schedule settings for the campaign.

pinpoint.campaign.SegmentId.Docs: The unique identifier for the segment to associate with the campaign.

pinpoint.campaign.SegmentVersion.Docs: The version of the segment to associate with the campaign.

pinpoint.campaign.Tags.Docs: An array of key-value pairs to apply to this resource.  For more information, see [Tag](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-properties-resource-tags.html).

pinpoint.campaign.TreatmentDescription.Docs: A custom description of the default treatment for the campaign.

pinpoint.campaign.TreatmentName.Docs: A custom name of the default treatment for the campaign, if the campaign has multiple treatments. A *treatment* is a variation of a campaign that's used for A/B testing.
